channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: When is an interview too tough? CBS News grappling with question after Dokoupil interview
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An internal debate over CBS News host Tony Dokoupil's pointed interview with author Ta-Nehisi Coates about Israel has spread to the boardroom, with the chief of the network's parent company saying Wednesday that it was a mistake to reprimand him.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: October Prime Day Dyson deals
item.description: There is no shortage in the selection of Dyson’s October Prime Day deals, but you must hurry before they are sold out.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, NewsNation

item.title: CBP beagle sniffs out bag carrying over 2 pounds of snake meat at Virginia airport
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection agricultural specialist beagle Freddie sniffed out a bag that contained over two pounds of snake meat carried into the country from West Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tren de Aragua gang member, illegal Venezuelan migrant, arrested in Houston
item.description: An illegal alien from Venezuela and a confirmed Tren de Aragua gang member was arrested in Houston on Monday in a joint effort between several law enforcement agencies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VIDEO: Hurricane hunters take turbulent trip through Milton
item.description: Researchers on a hurricane hunter aircraft had a bumpy ride as they flew into Hurricane Milton on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WGN 9

item.title: Video shows Floridians making last-minute preparations ahead of Hurricane Milton
item.description: Florida residents were seen making last-minute preparations such as filling sandbags and finding a spot to park their electric vehicle ahead of the powerful storm's arrival.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Beloved father fighting for life in ICU after house explosion in Long Beach
item.description: The man injured in a violent house explosion on Monday is fighting for his life in the intensive care unit, his family tells KTLA.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: Plane evacuating people from Hurricane Milton crashes after takeoff
item.description: A plane carrying passengers evacuating from Hurricane Milton crashed near the southeast corner of the St. Pete Pier Tuesday morning, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia university leaders ask NCAA to ban transgender women from sports
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The regents who govern Georgia's 26 public universities and colleges voted on Tuesday to ask the NCAA and another college athletic federation to ban transgender women from participating in women's sports. The unanimous vote came after Georgia Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A retired Houston officer gets 60 years in a couple's drug raid deaths that revealed corruption
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A former Houston police officer was sentenced to 60 years in prison on Tuesday for the murder of a married couple during a drug raid that revealed systemic corruption in the department’s narcotics unit and prompted criticism of the law enforcement agency and its tactics.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden sets 10-year deadline for US cities to replace lead pipes nationwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A decade after the Flint, Michigan, water crisis raised alarms about the continuing dangers of lead in tap water, President Joe Biden on Tuesday set a 10-year deadline for cities across the nation to replace their lead pipes, finalizing an aggressive approach aimed at ensuring that
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fentanyl hidden within carne asada beef seized in Fresno County
item.description: A Washington man has been arrested after a canine officer uncovered $500,000 worth of fentanyl hidden in carne asada beef packages and a tote cooler, the California Highway Patrol said.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Meza, Fox 5

item.title: Michigan US Senate debate: Rogers, Slotkin race could determine control
item.description: Rogers and Slotkin meet on Tuesday for an hour-long debate hosted by Nexstar's WOOD-TV, beginning at 7 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, KRON4

item.title: Las Vegas will blow a kiss goodbye — literally — to the Tropicana with a flashy casino implosion
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Sin City will quite literally blow a kiss goodbye to the Tropicana before first light Wednesday in an elaborate implosion that will reduce to rubble the last true mob building on the Las Vegas Strip. The Tropicana's hotel towers are expected to tumble in 22 seconds at 2:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform is addictive, harms kids' mental health
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia sued TikTok on Tuesday, alleging the platform exploits and harms young users while "deceiving" the public about these dangers.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Harris reveals owning a Glock: 'Of course' I've fired it
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris said in a "60 Minutes" interview that aired Monday that she owns a Glock and has fired it at a shooting range in another example of how she and her campaign have made a point to highlight her gun ownership.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton is a Category 5. Florida orders evacuations and scrambles to clear Helene's debris
item.description: BELLEAIR BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Florida’s storm-battered Gulf Coast raced against a Category 5 hurricane Monday as workers sprinted to pick up debris left over from Helene two weeks ago and highways were clogged with people fleeing ahead of the storm.
item.dc:creator: HAVEN DALEY and MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Korea's Kim again threatens to use nuclear weapons against South Korea and US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un warned again that he could use nuclear weapons in potential conflicts with South Korea and the United States, as he accused them of provoking North Korea and raising animosities on the Korean Peninsula, state media reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Raccoon sparks chaos in stands during Broncos game against Raiders
item.description: Football fans packed Empower Field in Denver on Sunday to watch the Broncos take on the Las Vegas Raiders, but they weren’t the only ones in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, Fox 5

item.title: Amid cyberattack, nation's largest water utility pauses billing
item.description: American Water provides services to more than 14 million people across 14 states and on 18 military installations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: When will we 'fall back?' What Utahns have tried to get daylight saving time permanent
item.description: It is almost time to fall back, the first weekend of November marks the end of daylight saving across much of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Hurricane Milton as it slams Florida's Gulf Coast
item.description: Powerful Hurricane Milton made landfall along Florida's Gulf Coast Wednesday night as a Category 3 storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: These cities are the 'safest' in America, new study says
item.description: The quality of schools, traffic and entertainment offerings are among the variety of factors you consider before moving to a new city. Another is safety.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: New analysis suggests national debt could increase under Harris, but it would surge under Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — No one is likely to be happy with the projected higher deficits laid out in a new analysis of Kamala Harris' and Donald Trump's economic plans.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Al Pacino claims he almost died of COVID: 'I was gone'
item.description: During a podcast appearance on The New York Times’ “The Interview," Al Pacino claims he once nearly died of COVID.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Live Wobble Tracker: Map shows real-time movements of Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton, which reached Category 5 strength Monday, is heading right for Florida's west coast, with a potential landfall near or at Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Colleges brace for Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: Colleges face a major test Monday as pro-Palestinian and pro-Israel students both hold events for the first anniversary of Hamas’s attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee nurse and his dog died trying to save a man from floods driven by Helene
item.description: “I’ve never had anyone risk their life for me,” David Boutin, the man Boone McCrary had set out to rescue said. “From what I hear that was the way he always been. He’s my guardian angel, that’s for sure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stenhouse snaps 65-race losing streak after late crash at Talladega scrambles playoff picture
item.description: Ricky Stenhouse Jr. snapped a 65-race losing streak by winning in overtime at Talladega on Sunday after a late crash collected more than half the field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: For small cities across Alabama with Haitian populations, Springfield is a cautionary tale
item.description: ENTERPRISE, Ala. (AP) — The transition from the bustling Port-au-Prince, Haiti, to a small Alabama city on the southernmost tip of the Appalachian mountain range was challenging for Sarah Jacques. But over the course of a year, the 22-year-old got used to the quiet and settled in.
item.dc:creator: SAFIYAH RIDDLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What happens to items surrendered to TSA?
item.description: The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) said it does not confiscate items. According to the agency, if someone shows up with a prohibited item, they are given a choice on what to do with it.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dolly Parton, Walmart announce millions in donations to Helene victims
item.description: East Tennessee's own Dolly Parton is expected to offer her assistance to help with the flood recovery efforts in Newport.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, WGN 9

item.title: Satellite shows before-and-after flooding in North Carolina after Helene
item.description: Planet Labs captured and compared satellite images that show the storm's devastating impact on one of the hardest hit cities.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald's adding Chicken Big Mac to US menus for a limited time
item.description: McDonald's is set to introduce the Chicken Big Mac, a reimagined version of its classic Big Mac, featuring two tempura-battered chicken patties on a sesame seed bun, on Oct. 10.
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, WGN 9

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15 to allow time to negotiate new contract
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports has reached a deal to suspend their strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract, a person briefed on the matter says.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
